title: Women at the Helm: Redefining India’s Development Story
content:
India’s development narrative has decisively shifted from empowering women to being powered by women. From classrooms to cutting-edge research labs, women are claiming their space in STEM with 43% enrolment and over 53% research fellowships. As India moves toward Viksit Bharat 2047, Nari Shakti is now the engine of economic growth.

Key Strides in Women-Led Development

Political Empowerment: The Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam (2023) ensures 33% reservation in legislatures, while over 14.5 lakh women lead at the grassroots level.

Economic Transformation: The Lakhpati Didi initiative has empowered over 2 crore women to earn sustainable incomes through sectors like drone tech, organic farming, and renewable energy.

STEM & Innovation: Programs like WISE-KIRAN and Vigyan Jyoti are strengthening women’s role in science, with 18.6% participation in R&D.

Financial Inclusion: Through Jan Dhan Yojana, 56% of bank accounts belong to women, ensuring financial independence and digital empowerment.

Workforce Growth: Female labour force participation has reached 35.3%, marking a significant structural shift.

Key Challenges

Unpaid Care Burden: Women perform 84% of unpaid work, contributing nearly 15–17% of GDP without recognition.

Limited Capital Access: Women-led startups receive only ₹4 out of every ₹100 in funding.

Workplace Inequality: The leaky pipeline and pay gaps persist, with India ranked 131st in Gender Gap Index 2025.

Informal Workforce Trap: Nearly 94% of women work in informal sectors with low security.

Digital Risks: Rising cyber harassment and AI bias limit women’s digital participation.

Way Forward

India must invest in gender-responsive urban planning, expand SHE-Mart cooperatives, and introduce returnship programs. Strengthening care infrastructure, climate-resilient agri-tech, and digital safety frameworks will unlock women’s full potential.

Women-led development is not just a goal—it is the foundation of India’s future growth.
